abstract_ts: As the Federal Republic of Germany is a federal state, powers and responsibilities under the federal constitution (Grundgesetz) are usually decentralized and divided between the federation (Bund) and the constituent states (Laender), and so is the power of constitutional review by constitutional courts. Under federal law, a citizen can raise a complaint to the Federal Constitutional Court (Bundesverfassungsgericht) claiming that an act of state violates his civil or political rights (Grundrechte) granted by the federal constitution. Some Laender have implemented similar provisions, empowering their constitutional courts to grant specific writs against infringements of civil rights guaranteed by the state's constitution. This brings up the difficulties of concurring jurisdiction, because a complaint can be based on rights contained in the federal as well as in a state constitution. In particular, criminal law and criminal procedure are almost completely regulated by federal legislation, but applied and executed primarily by authorities and courts of the Laender. Thus, as federal law is superior to state law, what limited scope remains for state courts to apply the state constitution? Can constitutional courts of the Laender reverse a decision based on federal law? The latter question was decided by the Federal Constitutional Court in 1997: in principle they can. According to that precedent, a complaint to a state constitutional court against a state court's decision based on federal procedural law is admissible if the state constitution grants the same protection as corresponding rights of the federal constitution. Furthermore, federal law must leave some scope for the state's authorities and courts to fill in at their discretion and in conformity with the spirit of the respective state's own constitutional values. The application of a state constitution also enables the constitutional courts of the Laender to exercise their original power of constitutional review. This essay discusses various provisions on criminal law in different state constitutions, e.g. like due process clauses, safeguards against unreasonable searches and seizures, or the guarantee of a fair hearing. The paper examines if and how far those guarantees correspond with federal law, which provisions of the federal criminal law leave room to apply constitutional rights of the Laender and, finally, under what circumstances an issue of criminal procedure can be brought before a state constitutional court. It is shown that, on the one hand, there is a broad scope of procedural matters left to the state authorities' discretion, on the other hand, the strict limitation of constitutional review, mentioned above, hinders the development of original aspects of a state's constitution and therefore is just another expression of the German phenomenon of the federal constitution's overwhelming centripetal force.

abstract_ts: The limits of EU competences are governed by the principle of conferral. Under this principle, the Union shall act only within the limits of the competences conferred upon it by the Member States in the Treaties to attain the objectives set out therein (Art. 5 para. 1 TEU). That means legislative competence in the field of spatial planning can only be based upon an accordant provision in the Treaties allowing binding regulations for Spatial Planning in the Member States. The European Treaties do not provide any Union competence that would empower the EU to release binding acts addressed to the Member States: Neither Art. 192 para. 1 TFEU nor Art. 192 para. 2 no. b first indent TFEU would allow for secondary legislation in the field of spatial planning. Art. 192 Para. 2 no. b first indent TFEU, authorising the adoption of measures affecting town and country planning in accordance with a special legislative procedure, refers to the Union policy on the environment but not to specific measures directly targeting spatial planning. The Lisbon Treaty did not expand the Union competences in the field of spatial planning, particularly not with regard to the explicit incorporation of "territorial cohesion" as an objective and area of shared competence. In consideration of the genesis of the objective "territorial cohesion" and its meaning within European Structural Policy, it cannot be approved that territorial cohesion and spatial planning are partially congruent. The concept of spatial planning differs from the perception of spatial resp. territorial development but conforms to the definition of spatial planning as defined by the German Raumordnungsgesetz. According to Art. 4 para. 1 and Art. 5 para. 2 TEU, the competence for both legislation on and implementation of spatial planning remains with the Member States.

abstract_ts: One of the basic pillars of German administrative procedural law is the inquisitorial principle (Untersuchungsgrundsatz), which obliges administrative courts to determine the relevant facts of a case ex officio. The inquisitorial principle determines even the procedure in general courts of law in competition law cases when a court has to decide whether a challenged administrative measure of the competent competition authority is in conformity with the law. Disregarding this, nowadays most administrative measures of the Federal Cartel Office (Bundeskartellamt) are based on European competition law (Article 101-102 TFEU), which is indirectly enforced by national authorities in the decentralised system of competition law enforcement established under Regulation 1/2003/EC. In contrast to the German administrative procedural law, the procedure in the European courts (European Court of Justice and, in first instance, General Court) is more or less based on the (adversarial) principle that evidence of the facts of the case has to be provided by the competing parties. This leads to fundamentally different modes of administrative procedure (not least the duration of a trial), in particular, in economically complex fields of law like competition cases. This essay analyses what the inquisitorial principle is really about and how the different courts involved in the enforcement of competition law deal with the problem of investigating the facts. Finally, the essay argues that the inquisitorial principle should be modified under the influence of European administrative law to make the procedural law more effective, from which the enforcement of competition law and likewise the legal protection of the claimants challenging administrative acts could benefit.

abstract_ts: The article deals with the question whether the Fundamental Rights, expressed in the German Basic Law (Grundgesetz), are legally binding for the German Representative in the Council of the European Union in the consultation process and decisions on European Laws, i.e. directives and regulations (art. 249 EC). The author holds the view that the Fundamental Rights of the Grundgesetz do not apply to these European legislative procedures provided that European Fundamental Rights as found and applicated by the European Court of Justice guarantee a similar standard of protection of liberty and equality. This view consequently extends the so-called "Solange" doctrine, which has been formulated in several famous decisions of the Federal Constitutional Court (Bundesverfassungsgericht, especially: BVerfGE 73, 339 [387], confirmed in BVerfGE 102, 147 ff.), to the matter of deliberating and finally deciding on European laws within the Council. This is at least the case in a certain interpretation of the "Solange" doctrine, which, though not beyond dispute, is favoured by the author: In this interpretation, "Solange" suggests a substantive, not only judicial constitutional rule, whereby German Fundamental Rights are not applicable to secondary Community Law and also not to German transformatory and executive Law determined by European legal acts, providing that European Fundamental Rights in the hands of national and European courts guarantee a standard of protection, which does not necessarily need to be identical but in general of a similar quality as the standard of protection warranted by the Fundamental Rights of the Grundgesetz. This doctrine, i.e. a constitutional stipulated rule of applicability, can be read as expressed in art. 23 para. 1 of the Grundgesetz, included in the written text of the Constitution in 1992. The author discusses and rejects the most important arguments which legal writers have been established in favour of their thesis of the applicability of constitutional Fundamental Rights with regard to the voting of German representatives in the Council. In particular, it is neither necessary nor reasonable to uphold a strong interdependency between the non-applicability of the Fundamental Rights as warranted in the Grundgesetz and the concept of supremacy of Community Law, as the latter can not put aside contrary national regulations before not being in force and applicable itself. Not very convincing either is the key-argument put forward by the vast majority of legal writers, that national representatives in the Council are preventionally bound to the Fundamental Rights of their national constitutions because these rights do not require any legal liability, once European legal acts have been put into force within the procedure of legislation according to the Treaty's rules. The most important counter-argument can be found in the constitutional rule expressed in art. 23 para. 1 Grundgesetz: Referring to the "Solange" -Doctrine of the Bundesverfassungsgericht art. 23 provides for a guarantee of a "generally similar protection of Fundamental Rights" within the Order of Community Law – of course not with binding character with regard to the organs of the Community but as a legal condition that must be fulfilled so that applicability of the Constitution's Fundamental Rights is to be withdrawn in favour of the applicability of Community's Fundamental Rights. This constitutional provision of a structurally equivalent protection of individual rights in the community order would be in no way understandable if constitutional Fundamental Rights were applicable at the same time. Specific objections are raised against the opinion of most legal writers that applicability of German Fundamental Rights is not given in a strict manner but rather characterised as more or less weak and contingent with regard to the decisions of the German Government in the European legislative process. The article concludes with a short outlook on the problem of how to understand and interpret the constitutional condition of an equivalent standard of human right's protection in Europe.

abstract_ts: The doctrine that rules must be clear and definite (doctrine of definiteness), can be derived from many principals of the German constitution. So the doctrine of definiteness originates from the rule of law (Rechtsstaatlichkeit) and its subprinciples of legal certainty and legislative-reservation-clause (Vorbehalt des Gesetzes) with its roots also in the democracy-principle, and also arises from the supremacy of basic rights. The doctrine of definiteness aims primarily at the legislator, however the demanded degree of definiteness can not be specified absolutely: It depends on the difficulties with formulating precisely the respective statute, as well as on the need for legal certainty of the persons within the applicability of the rule. This need is high, when statutes limit the basic rights. In any case the legal practice must be able to solve the given problems of interpretation. Therefore the doctrine of definiteness aims also at the judiciary which at least has to reduce the remaining uncertainties of the statute. If the application of precedents on similar sets of facts leads to diverging results not only occasionally this may indicate an infringement of the doctrine of definiteness. In the past the Federal Constitutional Court has enforced the doctrine of definiteness just to a small extent by declaring statutes invalid due to their lack of definiteness only in extreme cases. In its more recent judgments the Court has applied the doctrine of definiteness more strictly; however, its judgments are still nearly unpredictable. The doctrine of definiteness, which is to serve legal certainty, must be applied in a more „definite“ and predictable way itself. For this purpose the reflection on the doctrine's derivation from the basic rights seems to be useful. The indefiniteness of a statute that limits basic rights represents an additional (factual) encroachment on basic rights. Therefore, if a statute does not fulfill the attainable degree of definiteness, this must be justified by the specific need for statutory flexibility in the respective legislative field. It is for the judiciary to specify those legal concepts then remaining in need of interpretation. In this process also judiciary generally must choose the method which guarantees the maximum of forseeability, i.e. develop definitions for concepts which can be outlined more precisely by forming subdefinitions. Far less legal certainty, however, is awarded by a method that itemizes criteria which are significant for the compliance with the requirements of the legal concept, but does not provide for unambiguous instructions how to decide, if some of the criteria are fulfilled and some not. Therefore the judiciary has to apply the first method whenever possible. Legal concepts which are in extreme need of interpretation (i.e. general clauses) may be structured in different sets of cases each of which is handled by one of the two methods mentioned.

abstract_ts: Hemangiomas are relatively common in the head and neck region, but their occurrence in the temporal bone are extremely rare. The hemangioma of the external auditory canal (EAC) is a rare otologic entity as only 22 cases of hemangioma of the EAC have been reported worldwide. Here we report a patient presenting with pulsatile tinnitus, which turned out to be caused by capillary hemangioma arising from the posterior medial portion of external auditory canal touching the tympanic membrane. Surgical excision of the tumor was successful. The clinical manifestations and management of benign vascular lesions of the EAC are discussed with a review of literature. Key words: External auditory canal ã Hemangioma ã Tinnitus
Affiliations: Hallym University

abstract_ts: Since public services or services of general economic interest (e.g. telecommunications, railroad traffic, energy) - once performed by the state itself or by public authorities - have been continuously privatised within the last decade, European states have changed more and more to the shape and function of Gewährleistungsstaat, that is, a state which leaves performance (Erfüllung) of the services to the market but is nevertheless still obliged to secure this performance, as guaranteed by European and domestic law, by means of administrative regulation. The concept of Gewährleistungsstaat is based on the assumption, that the accomplishment of goals of adequate supply from the constitutional provision of welfare state or from EC Law, regularly is a side effect from private company's economic activities being first of all directed at making as much money as possible. This assumption can be regarded as proved by impressive improvements of quality and price cuts that have taken place in several sectors of privatised services during the last years, adherent risks which shouldn't be neglected as insignificant notwithstanding. At least some expectations of the legal system and the state directed towards functioning markets as to private suppliers will fully provide for the legally guaranteed results could be disappointed. Even in the telecommunications sector, which is probably the most successfully privatised network economy of all, competitive structures have been already established to a large extent, such as risks of "market failures", i.e. of an undersupply of electronic communications network and services as measured by EC's Universal Service standard, cannot be certainly excluded. Also, availability of the new legal instruments of regulation to ensure the telecommunications universal service depends on conditions of a workable market. Just like with regard to other social and economic preconditions, regulatory law isn't able to guarantee these prerequisites itself. Even in the market of electronic communications services - a fortiori this could be the case in economically more complicate network economies - the modern Gewährleistungsstaat therefore cannot be considered to have abandoned its responsibility for the adequate performance of services provided by the law. The state would otherwise be regarded as bound to an obligation it wouldn't be able to comply with under all circumstances. Legal interpretations, therefore, as have been deduced from EC Law and German Constitutional law by legal writers, which see the powers of the Gewährleistungsstaat as confined to a regulatory control towards exclusively private markets which are to be strictly barred against any entrepreneurial engagements of public authorities are not convincing. As long as legal systems in Europe assign the State the role as guarantor for the performance of certain legally defined goods and services available to all consumers, this responsibility must be covered by adequately and competently. These should also include at least certain forms of participation of the State as a player within the market if this is the only possible way to achieve provided objectives. Of course, such interventions in the markets made by government subsidies or public undertakings must be compatible with the rules of competition and they ought to be subsidiary and only complementary to the enterprises of private actors.

abstract_ts: Aortoenteric fistula is an uncommun life-threatening condition which remains associated with significant morbidity and mortality. It can be primary (aneurysm, neoplasms, radiation therapy, infection) or secondary to vascular prosthesis. Early diagnosis and aggressive surgical treatment are very important to achieve optimal outcomes in these patients. The aim of this article is to highlight the importance of early diagnosis and multidisciplinary approach of aortoenteric fistula through the presentation of a clinical case.

abstract_ts: Extensive study in fields like expert systems and unforeseen breakthroughs that have an impact on our regular activities are changing the world as we know. Researchers continuously develop novel, cutting-edge concepts, and paradigms as well as outstanding technologies that speedily mature and are released onto the market with applications using machine learning, pervasive social gaming, forecasting based on intricate network time series, simulated annealing projection pursuit model and nested fuzzy evaluation model, and expert system and entropy-OWA method. New and emergent technologies are changing the data management processes for better decision-making in several contexts, supply chains, education, cutting-edge regional innovations, and in industry in general. Expert systems are per si fundamental for decision-making processes. Numerous authors have investigated decision-making processes; according to Dhami et al. (2015) and Curseu and Schruijer (2012), it can be thought of as a conceptual process by which individuals choose from a range of choices. However, the integration with expert systems allows for the acquisition of real-time information that is necessary for tracking system status and for developing feasible alternatives (Church et al., 2016; Sabherwal & King, 1995). In this situation, the strategic potential of expert systems supports decision-making processes in problem definition, information identification, potential alternative development using simulations and forecasting models, alternative analysis using machine learning systems to make more effective decisions, best alternative selection with pervasive systems, decision implementation, and establishment of a control and evaluation system with nested fuzzy models. Decision-support expert systems apply logical models for better decision-making processes (Church et al., 2016; Huber, 1981; Song et al., 2017). New expert systems built on artificial intelligence and big data technologies present new difficulties in decision-making while providing limitless opportunities for interaction and integration to supplement or supplant human decision-makers (Duan et al., 2019). Interoperability of expert systems is one of the major issues, and distributive model-driven platforms for the parallel coordination of many discussions in data-intensive collaborative working environments can help to some extent. However, previous research indicates that collaboration expert systems can enhance teams' exchange of information (McNamara et al., 2008), potentiating more effective decisions. The six research articles in this special issue contribute to the development of this scientific discussion and analyse the ways expert systems contribute to facilitating and improving the operational decision-making processes (Fischer et al., 2015; Fox & Poldrack, 2014; Frederick, 2005). All submissions are revised and extended versions of articles that were originally presented at WorldCIST'21, the ninth World Conference on Information Systems and Technologies, which took place in Terceira Island, Azores, Portugal, from 30 March to 1 April 2021. The WorldCIST conference is already a well-established international forum for researchers and practitioners to present and discuss the most recent technologies, trends, results, experiences, and concerns in a number of areas of information systems and technologies, as well as computer science in general. The six selected papers in this special section include the following: Carneiro et al. (2023) present a study that discusses the use of meta-learning to predict performance metrics in Machine Learning problems. They point out the numerous difficulties faced by machine learning, many of which are due to the novel aspects, such as learning from streaming data or adding user input into already-existing datasets and models. According to their study data in these dynamic situations alter over time, necessitating model adaptation, and new data, however, may not always indicate fresh patterns. The major objective of this research is to develop a method to forecast a model's performance metrics before it is trained. To address this problem, they suggest using meta-learning, comparing two alternative meta-models, one created for a particular machine learning problem and the other created based on a variety of problems and intended to be a general meta-model usable for almost any problem. The methodology used is the root mean square error prediction (RMSE), and the findings demonstrate that, under streaming settings, it is possible to correctly anticipate the RMSE of future models. Leite et al. (2023) studied a model for determining patent revenue odds, focused on technology transfer offices (TTO), with the aim to identify which characteristics related to their human resources, influence obtaining revenue from the patents that constitute their portfolios. To achieve this objective, the authors analysed data from 272 TTO in Brazil, using the Logistic Regression technique, based on Maximum Likelihood Estimation. As a remarkable conclusion that emerges from the results is that the universities to increase their revenue must invest in full-time employees, and foster the inventions' communications, as well as to attract and retain employees with skills directly related to knowledge fields such as Law, Engineering, and Communication. Valera-Aranguren et al. (2023) present an experience of building a pervasive social gaming using Social PG. According to the study pervasive computing has become an important component in the development of applications that use fun as a motivator because it allows for the exploration of new interaction schemes by making the concept of space and time ambiguous and confusing. The study describes a pervasive social gaming experience by referencing SocialPG, a model that describes social expansion as a strategy for improving gaming experiences enabled by pervasive computing. The authors describe a model and suggest a software architecture to support it, a case study related to the process of error resolution and detection of improvement opportunities in software products is developed, and finally, a general idea about the final software product that will support the game experience is offered along with an evaluation carried out by a group of users, where some key findings are highlighted, such as the importance of a software product's ability to detect and fix errors in software products. Zhang (2023) presents a model for research and forecasting based on intricate network time series A modern complex network evolution research focuses on evaluating the macro-level changes in network structure's features to fill in a lack in the study of fundamental mechanisms of network structure change and the micro-level prediction of network structure. The authors state that the usual macro-level analysis and research methods are normally invalid for some complicated network time series, and the micro-level analysis method must be used instead. The article shows an investigation of the structural development trends of complex network time series and makes a forecast, this research builds a time series network analysis and prediction algorithm based on the evolution characteristics of nodes and edges in complex networks. This paper builds the enterprise supply–demand network time series using the data from the enterprise supply–demand relationship. Then, we analyse and forecast complicated network time series using the method put out in this research, therefore confirming its logic and viability. Ge and Lin (2023) make an evaluation of China's regional innovation capability based on a simulated annealing projection pursuit model and nested fuzzy evaluation model. The authors intend to establish an evaluation index system for regional innovation capacities to better understand regional variances and promote innovation. They use a combination of simulated annealing optimized projection pursuit (SA-PP) and N-layer nested fuzzy comprehensive evaluation models to assess China's regional innovation capabilities. The results of this study show that the SA-PP model is effective in lowering the risk of erroneous evaluation outcomes caused by index weight uncertainty, resulting in a more precise, dependable, and informative evaluation of regional innovation capabilities. The total score of the suggested combinational evaluation model shows that the provinces with the highest regional innovation potential are mainly concentrated in the southeast coastal regions. Shen and Qian (2023) analyse the expert system and entropy-OWA method in cold supply chain risk evaluation as the existing methods for evaluating cold supply chain risk lack systematic classification and quantitative analysis of the influences of risk elements in the chain. This study builds a knowledge base of expert systems based on expertise and extensive experience and modifies the expert weights in conjunction with entropy weights to reduce subjective error. The authors use an ordered weighted average (OWA) operator to efficiently analyse risk in a cold supply chain. A case study of a typical fresh food e-commerce company is analysed to highlight the risks in a food refrigeration supply chain. In addition, it was consulted an expert group with in-depth knowledge and competence, to build a knowledge base for the expert system, to assign risks, and updating the expert weight in conjunction with the entropy weight technique. The successful evaluation of the modified risk assignment is followed by the derivation and ranking of the risk value based on the OWA operator. The findings of the calculation are used to suggest particular risk mitigation measures. The results show that an evaluation methodology that combines an expert system knowledge base with the entropy-OWA method may effectively portray and process the assessment data necessary for risk rankings as well as solve supply chain risk issues more quickly and effectively. The guest editors would like to thank the reviewers for their thoughtful and in-depth comments and acknowledge the outstanding contributions made by researchers and practitioners to this special issue. The guest editors also credit Jon Hall, Editor-in-Chief of the Wiley journal Expert Systems. The program committee members for WorldCIST'21 are especially deserving of praise for their tireless efforts and dedication. Maria José Sousa (PhD in Management with Habilitation in Management and Public MAnagement) is Pro-Rector for the Development of Distance Learning and a professor and a research fellow at ISCTE/Instituto Universitário de Lisboa. She is also an expert in digital education and digital skills, as she has assumed a post-Doc position from 2016 to 2018, researching that field, with several publications in journals with high impact factors (Journal of Business Research, Journal of Grid Computing, Future Generation Computer Systems, and others). Worked as an expert, in a project of the European Commission for the creation of a new category regarding digital skills to be integrated with the European Innovation Scoreboard (EIS). She was a member of the Coordinator Committee of the Ph.D. in Management at Universidade Europeia. She was also a Senior Researcher at GEE (Research Office) in the Portuguese Ministry of Economy, responsible for Innovation, Research, and Entrepreneurship Policies, and a Knowledge and Competencies Manager at AMA, IP, Public Reform Agency (Ministry of the Presidency and the Ministers Council). She was also a Project Manager at the Ministry of Labour and Employment, responsible for Innovation, and Evaluation and Development of the Qualifications Projects. Her research interests currently are public policies of Innovation and Education. She is a best-seller author in Research Methods, ICT and People Management and has co-authored over 100 articles and book chapters in high-level journals (as the European Planning Studies, Information Systems Frontiers, Systems Research, and Behavioural Science, Computational and Mathematical Organization Theory, Future Generation Computer Systems, and others), and is the guest-editor of more than five Special Issues from Elsevier and Springer. Is the former President of the ISO/TC 260—Human Resources Management, representing Portugal in the International Organization for Standardization. She was/is the coordinator of several European projects of innovation (funded by programs such as Erasmus +, Horizon Europe, and ICMPD) and is also External Expert of COST Association—European Cooperation in Science and Technology. Álvaro Rocha holds the title of Honorary Professor, and holds a D.Sc. in Information Science, Ph.D. in Information Systems and Technologies, M.Sc. in Information Management, and BCs in Computer Science. He is a Professor of Information Systems at the University of Lisbon—ISEG, a researcher at ADVANCE (the ISEG Centre for Advanced Research in Management), and a collaborator researcher at both LIACC (Laboratory of Artificial Intelligence and Computer Science) and CINTESIS (Center for Research in Health Technologies and Information Systems). His main research interests are maturity models, information systems quality, online service quality, requirements engineering, intelligent information systems, e-Government, e-Health, and information technology in education. He is also Vice-Chair of the IEEE Portugal Section Systems, Man, and Cybernetics Society Chapter, and Editor-in-Chief of both JISEM (Journal of Information Systems Engineering & Management) and RISTI (Iberian Journal of Information Systems and Technologies). Moreover, he has served as Vice-Chair of Experts for the European Commission's Horizon 2020 Program, and as an Expert at the COST—intergovernmental framework for European Cooperation in Science and Technology, at the European Commission's Horizon Europe Program, at the Government of Italy's Ministry of Universities and Research, at the Government of Latvia's Ministry of Finance, at the Government of Mexico's National Council of Science and Technology, at the Government of Polish's National Science Centre, and at the Government of Cyprus's Research and Innovation Foundation.

abstract_ts: A avaliação integrada da exposição a agentes químicos às condicionantes sociais, econômicas e ambientais possibilita ações efetivas e incorporadas ao espaço social, como pressupõe a metodologia de “Avaliação de Saúde Pública” da Agência de Registro de Substâncias Tóxicas e Controle de Doenças (ATSDR) e à semelhança da abordagem Ecosaúde. Assim, nosso objetivo é descrever uma experiência de avaliação de saúde pública em comunidades de agricultores familiares expostos a agroquímicos em São José de Ubá, estado do Rio de Janeiro (RJ). A sociedade civil e a esfera pública foram envolvidas no processo que reuniu informações históricas, socioeconômicas, culturais, ambientais e de saúde. A exposição ocupacional e/ou ambiental aos toxicantes ocorre em níveis que ocasionam efeitos deletérios à saúde, exigindo ações, como a prática agroecológica, educação em saúde e ambiente e requalificação dos profissionais de serviços de saúde. A abordagem tem como benefício situar o sujeito como agente integrador e influenciador de seus processos, potencializando seu empoderamento para a uma gestão ecossistêmica conjunta.

abstract_ts: Penelitian ini bertujuan untuk mengetahui ada atau tidaknya pengaruh model pembelajaran berbasis masalah terhadap literasi sains peserta didik kelas X pada materi pokok larutan elektrolit dan non-elektrolit. Metode yang digunakan adalah metode quasi experiment dengan rancangan penelitian post-test only control group design. Populasi penelitian adalah seluruh peserta didik kelas X MIA MAN 2 Model Mataram. Sampel dalam penelitian ini adalah peserta didik kelas X MIA 1 and X MIA 4 MAN 2 Model Mataram.Teknik pengambilan sampel dilakukan dengan teknik cluster random sampling. Hasil penelitian menunjukkan bahwa literasi sains peserta didik pada materi larutan elektrolit dan non-elektrolit dengan menggunakan model pembelajaran berbasis masalah menghasilkan nilai rata-rata posttest 76,17. Sedangkan pada kelas yang menggunakan metode konvensional menghasilkan nilai rata-rata posttest 66,75. Pengujian hipotesis dalam penelitian ini menggunakan rumus uji-t sparated varians. Hasil uji-t pada taraf signifikan 5% diperoleh nilai thitung(4,14) ââ°¥ ttabel (1,671), yang berarti H0 ditolak dan Ha diterima. Hal perhitungan tersebut menunjukan bahwa penerapan model pembelajaran berbasis masalah (PBM) memberikan pengaruh yang lebih baik terhadap literasi sains peserta didik kelas X pada materi pokok larutan elektrolit dan non-elektrolit dibandingkan model pembelajaran konvensional.

abstract_ts: RESUMO: Efectuámos um estudo transversal que abarcou a totalidade dos doentes falecidos na nossa Unidade, de Abril de 1999 a Abril de 2001.Analisámos: 1) características clínico-epide miológicas referentes à idade, sexo, raça, classificação dos casos de tuberculose (baseada no sistema de notificação da OMS), dias de internamento, número de tratamentos antibacilares prévios e forma de tuberculose; 2) grupos nosológicos, de acordo com infecção VIH e/ou tuberculose multirresistente (TBMR) concomitante, patologia associada, complicações e causa de morte; 3) parâmetros laboratoriais à data da admissão (concentração de hemoglobina, neutrófilos totais, leucócitos totais, crea- tinina, TGO, TGP, LDH, amilase, albumina, pH, PaO2, PaCO2, CD4, carga viral e resultados do teste de sensibilidade aos antibacilares).Foram revistos 32 processos clínicos que se distribuíram segundo 4 grupos: VIH(+)/TB-11 (34,4%); VIH(+)/TBMR-7 (21,9%); TB-10 (31,3%); TBMR-4 (12,4%). Em relação à patologia associada contabilizámos 18 casos de SIDA (56,2%), 6 com doença neoplásica e/ou imunossupressão âminorâ (18,8%) e 5 casos de doença pulmonar crónica (15,6%). A causa de morte foi atribuída a uma complicação não relacionada com a tuberculose ou patologias associadas em 18 doentes (56,2%), à SIDA em 7 casos (21,9%) e à TB também em 7 doentes (21,9%).Concluímos que o grupo cuja causa de morte foi a TB se caracterizou por ser constituído por doentes de escalão etário superior, representando casos novos a que não se associaram complicações; pelo contrário, no grupo cuja causa de morte foi a SIDA figuravam os casos de retratamento por interrupção da terapêutica antibacilar, em doentes maioritariamente mais jovens. Níveis elevados de TGO associaram-se significativamente às complicações relativas ao aparelho cardiovascular (tromboembolismo pulmonar e arritmias), assim como os valores médios mais baixos de hemoglobina se associaram às complicações de natureza infecciosa (pneumonia nosocomial, sépsis e infecção oportunista).REV PORT PNEUMOL 2002; VIII (3): ABSTRACT: Objective: To analyze the clinical and epidemiological characteristics associated with morta-lity following the in-hospital diagnosis of tuberculosis (TB).Design: Descriptive study.Setting: Tuberculosis Unit (Pulido Valente Hospital), Lisbon, Portugal.Method: The records of all patients died between April 1999 and April 2001 were reviewed to ascertain whether there had been significant differences regarding demographic factors, WHO notification categories and prior treatment history according to Multidrug-resistant tuberculosis, HIV status, comorbid conditions, cause of death and laboratory parameters.Results: The 32 study patients were divided into 4 groups: HIV/TB-11 (34,4%), HIV/MDR-TB - 7 (21,9%), TB- 10 (31,3%) and MDR-TB - 4 (12,4%). There were 6 cases of associated neoplastic disease (18,8%) and 5 patients with COPD (15,6%). The cause of death was attributed to AIDS or TB in 14 patients (43,8%) and unrelated to tuberculosis in 18 patients (56,2%).Conclusions: Our data suggest that TB as a cause of death was associated with advanced age, in patients with no prior treatment history and without hospital complications. On the contrary, the group which death resulted from AIDS was formed by younger patients with noncompliant prior treatments. A significant increase of GOT levels was associated with cardiovascular complications (acute pulmonary embolism and arrhythmia) and low hemoglobin concentration predicted infectious complications (nosocomial pneumonia and opportunistic infections).REV PORT PNEUMOL 2002; VIII (3) Palavras-chave: Tuberculose, mortalidade, tuberculose multirresistente, infecção VIH, Key-words: Tuberculosis, in-hospital mortality, multidrug-resistant tuberculosis, HIV infection

abstract_ts: Helium implantation-induced layer splitting of InP in combination with direct wafer bonding was utilized to achieve low temperature layer transfer of InP onto Si(1 0 0) substrates. InP(1 0 0) wafers with 4 inch diameter were implanted by 100 keV helium ions with a dose of 5 Ã 1016 cm−2. Then the as-implanted wafers were coated with a spin-on glass (SOG) oxide having a thickness of 150 nm. The SOG coated InP wafers were subsequently bonded to thermally oxidized Si(1 0 0) handle wafers and the bonded wafer pairs were annealed at 200 Â°C for 20 h to achieve InP layer transfer onto Si(1 0 0) wafers, enabling monolithic integration of InP with Si. Cross-sectional transmission electron microscope images of the transferred InP layers revealed that the layers were about 650 nm thick, which consisted of a heavily damaged InP layer about 300 nm thick directly at the surface and a remaining 350 nm thick layer with considerably less damage.

abstract_ts: O objetivo deste estudo é compreender a relação entre os diversos indicadores socioeconómicos globais e os casos positivos de doping no futebol em todos os países do mundo. Metodologicamente, recorremos a métodos quantitativos. Através de fontes oficiais, recolhemos os dados de todos os indicadores socioeconómico previamente definidos e dos casos positivos de doping. Realizámos vários procedimentos estatísticos, designadamente estatística descritiva, testes de hipóteses, correlações e análise de Variância. Através dos procedimentos estatísticos, aferimos que os indicadores com maiores médias são o índice capital humano (65,05) e o índice de corrupção (41,93), significando que estes indicadores são os mais importantes para os países. Verificamos, que existem mais países sem casos positivos (n=126) do que países com casos positivos de doping (n=58). O Sig (2 extremidades) apresenta um p<0.05, logo, o teste de t de Student mostra diferenças estatisticamente significativas entre todas as variáveis analisadas e o número de casos de doping,à exceção da taxa de desemprego e do coeficiente de Gini para um intervalo de confiança de 95%. Concluímos que quanto maior o capital o humano e a perceção da corrupção menor o consumo de doping. Aferimos também que quanto maior o IDH e o índice de felicidade, maior o consumo de doping. A taxa de desemprego e o coeficiente de Gini não mostraram qualquer diferença estatística.

abstract_ts: We investigate the ionic current modulation in DNA nanopore translocation setups by numerically solving the electrokinetic mean-field equations for an idealized model. Specifically, we study the dependence of the ionic current on the relative length of the translocating molecule. Our simulations show a significantly smaller ionic current for DNA molecules that are shorter than the pore at low salt concentrations. These effects can be ascribed to the polarization of the ion cloud along the DNA that leads to an opposing electric dipole field. Our results for DNA shine light on the observed discrepancy between infinite pore models and experimental data on various sized DNA complexes.

abstract_ts: Esse texto aborda a temática da pobreza e da desigualdade sob o olhar dos organismos internacionais. A primeira parte destaca o processo de endividamento latino-americano, os efeitos dos programas de estabilização e ajuste estrutural e a crescente desigualdade. Na segunda, a atenção volta-se para o papel dos organismos internacionais ”“ PNUD, Banco Mundial, BID ”“ suas concepções, medidas e recomendações para o enfrentamento à pobreza. A apropriação desse debate reveste-se de grande importância, tendo em vista que, no momento de definir as estratégias de políticas públicas dirigidas para o atendimento às privações próprias da pobreza, os conceitos adotados refletirão os critérios que orientarão as ações das referidas políticas.

abstract_ts: Background and Objectives The aim of this study is to evaluate the clinical outcomes of two surgical techniques-modified Bondy technique and canal wall up mastoidectomy with tympanoplasty type I and scutumplasty (CWUM/T1)-to remove attic cholesteatoma while preserving ossicular chain intact. Subjects and Method A retrospective study was performed on 23 surgical cases for the attic cholesteatoma with postoperative audiometry data of more than six months after surgery. The patientsâ postoperative clinical features and audiometric results were compared between the two surgical groups. Results Out of 23 patients, CWUM/T1 was performed in 13 cases and modified Bondy technique was used in 10 cases. There were no significant differences for the preoperative and postoperative audiograms between the two groups. But air-bone gap increased significantly after CWUM/T1 while it decreased after modified Bondy technique. Three cases with postoperative problems were seen after CWUM/T1 (recurrent cholesteatoma, pars tensa adhesion, recurrent otitis media with effusion). Two cases with postoperative problems were found after modified Bondy technique (mild attic retraction, pars tensa retraction). Conclusion Both surgical techniques seem to be adequate to treat attic cholesteatoma while preserving intact ossicular chain. Given good postoperative hearing results and stability of open cavity against recidivism, the modified Bondy technique seems to be a good choice for the attic cholesteatoma with intact ossicular chain when mastoid is not highly pneumatized. Key words: Audiometry ã Cholesteatoma ã Mastoid ã Otologic surgical procedures

abstract_ts: Antecedentes: La diabetes se ha convertido en un problema mundial de salud pública que genera múltiples complicaciones y tiene un gran impacto en la calidad de vida de los pacientes. Objetivo: Determinar la calidad de vida en pacientes con diagnóstico de diabetes tipo 2 con y sin complicaciones antes y después de una intervención educativa. Método: Estudio cuasiexperimental, longitudinal, prospectivo. Se aplicó el cuestionario de calidad de vida en pacientes diabéticos DQOL (alfa de Cronbach 0.91), antes y después de una intervención educativa. Se utilizó la prueba estadística de rangos de Wilcoxon. Resultados: Participaron 157 pacientes con diabetes tipo 2, 111 sin complicaciones y 46 con complicaciones. La prueba de Wilcoxon reportó z: –8.279 (p < 0.05) para los que no tuvieron complicaciones y z: –5.823 (p < 0.05) para los que tenían complicaciones. Conclusión: Se demostró estadísticamente que el aplicar una intervención educativa mejoró la calidad de vida.

abstract_ts: Este artigo faz uma ponte entre as idéias de Boaventura de Sousa Santos, a respeito do papel da universidade pública na construção de um projeto de sociedade contrahegemônico à globalização capitalista, e as idéias de István Mészáros, a respeito da radicalidade de uma educação para além do capital. Na costura dessas duas visões da mudança social, utilizamos a visão de Edgar Morin sobre a natureza complexa dos paradigmas e as condições socioculturais de sua transformação. Indagam-se aqui as possibilidades de produção de um novo conhecimento enraizado nas lutas sociais pela superação da hegemonia da lógica do capital e de sua determinação sobre a produção material, cultural e educacional de nossa sociedade. A universidade pública é considerada como espaço privilegiado de democratização da produção do saber científico e de contribuição à construção de um projeto de sociedade revolucionário.
